title: Maybach To Launch a Timeshare-Like Experience at Sea in 2029
description: Maybach plans to launch its first branded superyacht in 2029 as an exclusive membership club. The Maybach Ocean Club will be a timeshare-like resort on the water for the world’s elite. Unlike a timeshare, clients will enjoy fractional owner
content:
The membership club, founded in partnership with Splendid Sea AG, is a first-of-its kind experience meant to transfer the ultra-luxury lifestyle that the Maybach brand brings to the open road to the water.
Maybach logo on the side of the Beyond Horizons superyacht. | Dölker + Voges Design
The brand, traditionally associated with the cars of its parent company Mercedes-Benz, has a yachting touchpoint in its history. In the 1920s, Maybach created engines that powered boats for the world’s leading shipbuilders.
Maybach Ocean Club will be aboard the custom-built, 155‑meter Beyond Horizons yacht by Dölker + Voges out of Hamburg, Germany. It will feature 30 state rooms, all equal in size (74-square meters), and six guest suites that will be available to guests of club owners.
Maybach Beyond Horizons superyacht exterior close up of decks. | Dölker + Voges Design
The Maybach Ocean Club is scheduled to be available to its owners 44 weeks of the year. Each of the selected 300 Ocean Club members will have access to their quarters four weeks annually. Additionally, they will be able to use the vessel as their own personal holiday respite should they be nearby, with the yacht serving as a day club for its members.
There will be about 15,000 square feet of public space and 40,000 square feet of open deck space. Part of that will include dining areas, a spa and gym facilities. A personal trainer will be available.
Additionally, guests may enjoy bespoke onshore experiences and curated moments such as truffle hunting in the Italian Piedmont or stargazing in Saint Lucia.
Maybach Beyond Horizons Superyacht dining room interior. | Heller Studios Design
Maybach’s yacht was designed in partnership with the Mercedes-Benz Design Team, led by Chief Design Officer Gorden Wagener. Its interior fuses hallmark design elements from the Maybach brand alongside upmarket textures, including significant upholstery moments throughout each of the guest cabins.
The crew of 85 will provide concierge-level service to those on board. And, a shadow vessel will follow the ship around the world, providing toys, tenders and a helicopter for use. It will also have additional staff quarters.
Club members, as fractional owners of the superyacht, will be able to vote on the yacht’s destinations. Splendid Seas plans to start with popular ports in the Mediterranean (Ibiza, Positano, Sardinia and Cannes, for example) then sail to the Caribbean. The Maldives, Seychelles and French Polynesia are within reach should the owners wish to go.
Maybach’s announcement follows the trend of luxury brands attaching their names to superyachts. Next year will mark the inaugural sailing of the Four Seasons yacht, which is longer and has three times the number of suites as the Beyond Horizons ship. Aman’s Aman at Sea arm will launch a 47-room luxury yacht, Amangati, in 2027. Orient Express Corinthian is a 54-cabin vessel, the world’s largest sailing yacht. It will have its first sail next summer.
Update 12/12/25, 2:08 p.m. ET: This article was updated for clarity.

title: Mets Targeting $182 Million Slugger After Edwin Diaz Whiff: Report
description: The New York Mets needed to make some big moves in free agency after whiffing on the postseason earlier this year, but they're trending in the wrong direction.
content:
The New York Mets needed to make some big moves in free agency after whiffing on the postseason earlier this year, but they're trending in the wrong direction.
NEW YORK, NEW YORK - JULY 23: Edwin Díaz #39 of the New York Mets pitches against the Los Angeles Angels during their game at Citi Field on July 23, 2...Read More | Al Bello/Getty Images
"The Mets clearly need to add another late-inning reliever, so Suarez is among the options that make sense," Feinsand wrote. "That said, whomever they sign won’t cost as much as Díaz, leaving some more money for them to pursue a reunion with Alonso.
"Losing both Díaz and Alonso -- two of their most popular players -- would certainly be a gut punch to the fan base. The sticking point between the Mets and Alonso could very well be years, as sources say that the Mets may be hesitant to go more than three years with the first baseman."
Alonso should be the team's top priority now. They can't afford to whiff on the postseason, lose Diaz, miss out on Kyle Schwarber, and then lose Alonso as well.
The Mets might be caught up in the length of the contract, but they need to bite the bullet and give him the money and deal that he wants. New York can't afford to lose Alonso because of a year or two in contract length.

title: In Minnesota’s Somali Community, Fear and Resolve After Trump Attacks
description: Minnesota’s large Somali community is fearful after President Donald Trump intensified his criticism of the community in recent days and Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) launched an operation in the region, members told Newsweek.
content:
Minnesota’s large Somali community is fearful after President Donald Trump intensified his criticism of the community in recent days and Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) launched an operation in the region, members told Newsweek.
“They contribute nothing. I don’t want them in our country,” Trump said of the community during a December 2 Cabinet meeting. “We can go one way or the other, and we’re going to go the wrong way if we keep taking in garbage into our country.”
Demonstrators rally outside a Target location on December 4, 2025 in Minneapolis. Local activists have accused Target of allowing ICE officers to stag...Read More | Getty Images/Stephen Maturen
Refugees from war-torn Somalia have been welcomed to Minnesota since the 1990s, drawn by job opportunities and the state’s generous social services. The state is home to nation’s largest Somali population, with about 84,000 people of Somali descent living in the Minneapolis-Saint Paul area.
In recent years, that community has had to reckon with the fallout from a huge welfare fraud scandal. Many but not all of the defendants in those cases are of Somali descent and most are U.S. citizens.
Those cases are being “used as an excuse to smear the entire Somali American community,” Abdulle, a former member of the New Brighton City Council, told Newsweek.
Kowsar Mohamed, a community organizer from south Minneapolis, called it a “red herring,” saying Trump was seizing on the fraud cases to distract people from the crises Americans are facing.
“We're dealing with Medicaid, Medicare challenges. We're dealing with food insecurity across the nation,” she said. “This community is being torn up from within to really create a level of forgetfulness around what is actually happening within our economy.”
“No particular community is homogeneous or a monolith,” she said, adding that it was “insane” to paint the entire community with the same brush.
Representative Hussein, said he would be the first person to call for those who committed fraud to face accountability, but said it was sad the scandal was being used to “diminish” the entire community.
On Thursday, the Department of Homeland Security said in a news release that agents with ICE had arrested “some of the worst of the worst criminal illegal aliens” since it launched Operation Metro Surge in the Minneapolis area. The news release listed seven people arrested, including three from Somalia.
Most people of Somali descent in Minnesota and the U.S. are U.S. citizens, according to the American Community Survey, an ongoing survey conducted by the Census Bureau.
A big concern, Mohamed said, is that they will be mistakenly detained by immigration agents and “then no one knows where they are.”
“That is the biggest concern for folks,” she said. “No one wants to get lost in the system.”
Abdulle said many in the community have started carrying passports. "Most of these people are U.S. citizens who are afraid to be targeted by ICE because of the color of their skin or their national origin,” he said. “This is unconstitutional, and it is un-American.”
Representative Hussein said ICE activity has increased in the past week, alleging that agents were racially profiling residents.
McLaughlin added: “What makes someone a target for immigration enforcement is if they are illegally in the U.S.—NOT their skin color, race, or ethnicity.”
She said that under the Fourth Amendment of the U.S. Constitution, agents use “reasonable suspicion” to make arrests. “If and when we do encounter individuals subject to arrest, our law enforcement is trained to ask a series of well-determined questions to determine status and removability,” she added.
He said it is “undeniable” that Somali immigrants in Minnesota have been successful, thriving in fields including business, health care, technology, education and local government. “I am one of those Somalis who have lived in the United States for almost 45 years, and I have educated so many American students,” he said.
The White House's Jackson said Minnesota Governor Tim Walz—Kamala Harris' running mate in the 2024 Election against Trump—has “allowed Somali refugees to turn Minnesota into a hub of fraudulent money laundering activity to fund lavish lifestyles overseas at the expense of American taxpayers. While the media and Democrats feign outrage, Americans who have suffered at the hands of these schemes will celebrate the President’s comments and strong support for AMERICAN citizens.”
A rise in the community’s political power helped the election of the first Somali American to Congress, Representative Ilhan Omar, and other Somalis have served in the state Legislature and on the Minneapolis and Saint Paul city councils.
Hussein said Trump’s attacks on the Somali community are a political tactic designed to sow division. “It's part of political strategy to target immigrants and communities of color when the polling numbers drop or when attention is slipping away,” he said.
It is just the latest instance of Trump “doubling down on empty, hateful rhetoric to divide the country,” Abdulle said.“He has done this so many times in the past by going after Mexican or Haitian immigrants, and unfortunately, I don’t think this will be the last time the president will spew hate and division.”
And Hussein said the community will stand firm and united. “Let’s be clear, we are nation built by immigrants. We are Minnesotan, we are Americans, and we are not going anywhere,” he said. “This is our home and we will face this challenge with unity, dignity and unwavering resolve.”

title: Most Registered Voters Think Second Boat Strike Was ‘Wrong’: Poll
description: Hegseth Questioned If He Will Release Video Of Second Strike On Alleged Drug Boat
content:
A recent Harvard CAPS-Harris Poll conducted December 2-4 surveyed 2,204 registered voters about military engagement with alleged drug boats in the Caribbean. The poll presented a scenario in which one was struck twice with missiles, as survivors of the first strike were killed by the second.
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has defended the strikes as "lawful under both U.S. and international law," but revelations about the vessel's destination have fueled legal and political scrutiny.
According to the Harvard CAPS-Harris Poll, 62 percent of registered voters prefer intercepting alleged drug boats over sinking them, with 38 percent supporting destruction. The partisan breakdown reveals stark divisions: 78 percent of Democrats favor interception compared to 40 percent of Republicans, while independents show 69 percent support for interception.
On whether launching a second strike that may kill survivors was appropriate, 54 percent believe it was wrong while 46 percent say it was right. Republicans showed strongest support at 74 percent, compared to just 24 percent of Democrats and 36 percent of independents supporting the action.
The poll was supervised by Mark Penn, chairman of The Harris Poll, Dritan Nesho, CEO of HarrisX, and Stephen Ansolabehere, professor and CAPS director emeritus at Harvard University. The survey has a margin of error of plus or minus 1.99 percentage points on a 95 percent confidence level.
U.S. Senator Jack Reed of Rhode Island, top Democrat on the Senate Armed Services Committee, in a statement Thursday:
"This briefing confirmed my worst fears about the nature of the Trump Administration’s military activities and demonstrates exactly why the Senate Armed Services Committee has repeatedly requested – and been denied – fundamental information, documents, and facts about this operation. This must and will be the only beginning of our investigation into this incident."
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, right, looks on as President Donald Trump speaks during a meeting of his Cabinet at the White House on December 2 in W...Read More
Lawmakers have called on the DOD to release full unedited video footage of the September missile attack to clarify the sequence of events and orders given.

title: NATO Ally Declares National Emergency Over Balloons
description: NATO member Lithuania has declared a national emergency over smuggling balloons crossing into its airspace from neighboring Belarus, a key ally of Russia.
content:
NATO member Lithuania has declared a national emergency over smuggling balloons crossing into its airspace from neighboring Belarus, a key ally of Russia.
The Lithuanian government said the balloons posed a "danger" to human life, property and the environment, and that the actions were seen as a form of hybrid warfare.
A Lithuanian border guard officer inspects a balloon used to carry cigarettes into the country from Belarus. (State Border Guard Service/AP)
Tensions between Lithuania and Belarus have increased in recent weeks due to meteorological balloons crossing from Belarus, which have forced the NATO member to repeatedly shut down its main airport.
The incursions add to concerns that hybrid operations along NATO's eastern flank could disrupt the region's security.
While Belarus has has not been involved directly in Moscow’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, it has been used a staging post for attacks.
Lithuania’s state of emergency was announced on Tuesday following a cabinet meeting of the government, which is a strong backer of Ukraine.
The declaration allows state institutions to coordinate their response and involve the military. The decision will be reviewed in a month, according to the country’s interior ministry.
Belarus' President Alexander Lukashenko - who is President Vladimir Putin's closest ally - accused Lithuania on Tuesday of escalating and politicizing the issue of weather balloons, playing down the safety fears that pilots face.
Belarusian opposition leader Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya, who ran against Lukashenko in 2020 in disputed elections that were followed a crackdown, condemned Minsk's "latest provocations."
"Threats to regional security will continue as long as the dictatorship remains," she wrote on X. "We stand firmly with Lithuania against these hybrid attacks."
Earlier this month, European Commission President Ursula Von der Leyen warned that the situation at the EU's border with Belarus was worsening, and that Brussels was preparing further sanctions to punish Minsk.
"Such hybrid attack by the Lukashenka regime is completely unacceptable," she wrote on X. "Lithuania continues to have our full solidarity."
The Institute for the Study of War (ISW) also said this month that the wave of balloon incursions was part of the "phase zero" strategy by Russia, whose leader the to pressure NATO through constant low-cost disruptions.
Update 12/09/25, 06.50 a.m. ET: This article was updated with further information.

title: Nancy Mace Says Pelosi ‘More Effective’ Than Any Republican
description: Republican Congresswoman Nancy Mace of South Carolina said she thinks that former Democratic Speaker of the House Nancy Pelosi was a "more effective" leader than any Republican has been "this century."
content:
Republican Congresswoman Nancy Mace of South Carolina said she thinks that former Democratic Speaker of the House Nancy Pelosi was a "more effective" leader than any Republican has been "this century."
Recent statements by Republican lawmakers, including Mace and Representative Marjorie Taylor Greene of Georgia, highlight significant turmoil and introspection within the House Republican caucus as the party faces impending retirements, and a looming midterm challenge.
Both GOP figures have publicly recognized Pelosi's effectiveness as a legislative leader—an uncommon acknowledgment amid deeply divided partisan politics. These remarks arrive as a wave of retirements threaten the party's razor-thin House majority, compounding existing fissures over policy and strategy under Johnson.
Mace said in part: "Here’s a hard truth Republicans don’t want to hear: Nancy Pelosi was a more effective House speaker than any Republican this century. I agree with her on essentially nothing. But she understood something we don’t: No majority is permanent. When Democrats hold the majority, they ram through the most progressive policies they can. They deliver for the coalition that elected them while they are in power."
"Republicans do the opposite. We get the majority, then become petrified of losing it. We pass the most moderate policies we can pressure conservatives to accept, betraying the coalition that delivered us here. Ms. Pelosi was ruthless, but she got things done," Mace added.
The South Carolina Republican also detailed how the current system lacks accountability and regularly sidelines rank-and-file voices. Mace wrote, “Leaders of both parties have systematically silenced rank-and-file voices.” She noted the collapse of open rules and meaningful amendment processes under multiple Congresses, emphasizing a trend that has spanned decades but has not been reversed—and has at times worsened—under the current leadership.
"Speaker Mike Johnson is better than his predecessor. But the frustrations of being a rank-and-file House member are compounded as certain individuals or groups remain marginalized within the party, getting little say," Mace said.
U.S. Republican Representative Nancy Mace of South Carolina walks off the House floor before a vote on the Republican budget plan at the U.S. Capitol ...Read More
Greene, who recently announced her retirement, went further in a televised interview. Appearing on CNN in November, Greene said, "I served under [Pelosi’s] speakership in my first term of Congress, and I’m very impressed at her ability to get things done. I wish we could get things done for our party like Nancy Pelosi was able to deliver for her party.” She acknowledged Pelosi’s “incredible career for her party” and also stated in part, “I wish her well in her retirement,” while noting that she wished politicians left Washington sooner.
Their remarks come amid notable discord within the Republican caucus. Several members—including Greene, along with Representatives Troy Nehls, Jodey Arrington and Elise Stefanik—have either announced retirements or decisions to seek other offices. Disagreements over government funding, health care reform and internal House rules have further exposed fractures in the GOP, as Johnson contends with public rebukes from both moderate and Trump-aligned members.
Columbia University professor Robert Y. Shapiro, to Newsweek via email on Monday:
"Mace had, for example, wanted Johnson to release the Epstein documents earlier, but he could not do that due to Trump's opposition. She doesn't give Johnson credit for getting all the legislation Trump has wanted through the House--especially the Big Beautiful Bill--whereas Pelosi was given full credit for all the legislation she accomplished, including the clever maneuvering to pass the Affordable Care Act, and later all of Biden's major budget legislation. Johnson now may get some blame from Mace and Trump if Republicans lose control of the House in the 2026 midterm elections."
The retirement of seasoned lawmakers and open intraparty criticism leaves House Republicans with a narrowing majority and an uncertain legislative future.

title: Polar Vortex To Hit Florida With Plunging Temperatures
description: A weakened polar vortex has allowed Arctic air to descend into the U.S. , bringing chilly temperatures as far south as Florida this week.
content:
A weakened polar vortex has allowed Arctic air to descend into the U.S., bringing chilly temperatures as far south as Florida this week.
According to forecasts from AccuWeather, the first wave of cold began sweeping through the Midwest earlier this week, with subzero temperatures forecast for states including North Dakota, Minnesota, Iowa and Nebraska. Meteorologists expect a second and third Arctic blast to follow over the next two weeks, bringing hazardous travel conditions and increased energy demands.
The cold weather has dropped as far south as Georgia and Florida, prompting freeze warnings and frost advisories for the Southern states. It's not abnormal for northern Florida to see frost this time of year, National Weather Service (NWS) meteorologist Paige Klingler told Newsweek
The polar vortex is a system of low pressure and cold air that typically circulates over Earth's poles. When stable, it contains frigid air far to the north. According to the NWS and multiple meteorological outlets, a stretch and weakening of this system has allowed Arctic air to spill southward, reaching as far as Florida.
A stock photo of a thermometer in cold weather. Frost advisories have been issued in Florida. | BeritK/Getty Images
Meteorologists anticipate multiple cold waves between December 10 and 19, impacting much of the Midwest, East Coast, and the Southeast, including North and parts of Central Florida.
The NWS office in Jacksonville issued a Frost Advisory for several North Florida counties from 3 a.m. to 8 a.m. EST on Wednesday. The advisory includes Suwannee, Baker, Union, Eastern and Western Hamilton, Northern, Southeastern and Southwestern Columbia counties. Temperatures as low as 33 degrees Fahrenheit are forecast, raising the risk of frost formation and harm to sensitive outdoor vegetation.
Forecasts for key Florida cities show notable temperature drops. For example, Tallahassee is expected to see lows of 37 degrees on Tuesday, while Jacksonville will experience lows in the lower 40s. Panhandle cities could see overnight lows dipping into the upper 20s to low 30s, particularly north of Interstate 10. Central and South Florida will also cool down, with Orlando expected to reach lows near 49 degrees Fahrenheit. Despite these declines, South Florida will remain above typical frost and freeze thresholds.
Meteorological forecasts anticipate at least two and possibly three more rounds of intense cold from the Midwest through the East, affecting Florida between December 10 and 19. The NWS urges residents in affected Florida counties to protect sensitive outdoor plants and take precautions during the advisory period.
Another cold front is expected to arrive by the following weekend, potentially bringing further frost or freeze events, especially in North Florida. Forecasters will continue to monitor the evolving pattern.
Travelers and residents should stay informed about forecast changes, particularly as storm tracks and cold wave durations remain uncertain. The potential for even colder temperatures and infrastructure challenges remains, and preparedness measures are recommended as this Arctic outbreak progresses.

title: Heritage Foundation 2025-2026 Priorities: Read in Full
description: The Heritage Foundation outlined its priorities for 2026 after Project 2025 received considerable backlash over its strategies for limited government, border security and tough immigration laws, among other conservative measures.
content:
The Heritage Foundation outlined its priorities for 2026 after Project 2025 received considerable backlash over its strategies for limited government, border security and tough immigration laws, among other conservative measures.
The list was originally published in February and recently gained traction on social media under the labeling Project 2026.
"Project 2025 was an effort of more than 100 organizations convened by Heritage to prepare for the next conservative administration. That administration is now in office, so all policy and personnel decisions are up to the president and his team," the spokesperson said, adding, "There is no Project 2026 and will not be, regardless of what leftist lunatics make up on BlueSky."
The priority list signals an ambitious effort to consolidate executive power, overhaul federal agencies, and redefine federal policy in alignment with conservative and Christian nationalist principles.
Analysts and advocates on all sides are watching closely, as the plan’s implementation could dramatically affect civil service staffing, agency independence, and key government programs—policies that will impact millions of Americans.
The etched sign that adorns the main entranceway to The Heritage Foundation headquarters in Washington, D.C., photographed on October 4, 2012. | Getty Images
The priorities listed for 2026 build directly off Project 2025, also known as the 2025 Presidential Transition Project, launched by The Heritage Foundation to provide a ready-to-implement policy manual, vetted personnel lists, and draft executive actions for the next Republican president.
The project’s central document, “Mandate for Leadership,” spans more than 900 pages and includes detailed plans to reorganize, reduce, or dismantle several federal agencies and programs. The overarching goal is to centralize presidential authority, eliminate perceived liberal influence from federal agencies, and implement widespread conservative changes across government, culture, energy, the economy, and health care.
The Heritage Foundation is a Washington, D.C.-based conservative think tank founded in 1973. It has been instrumental in advising Republican presidents and lawmakers for decades, notably through its “Mandate for Leadership” documents released ahead of U.S. presidential elections.
The foundation’s influence expanded under President Ronald Reagan and saw renewed prominence under the Trump administration, where it was credited with shaping key policies. It describes its mission as advancing conservative values, restoring what it sees as self-governance, and opposing liberal or progressive policies in government and culture.
Despite attempts by the Trump campaign to distance itself from the project, significant overlap in personnel and policy direction remains evident.
Many of the plan’s legislative recommendations, like shrinking the federal workforce, curtailing environmental regulations, and changing civil rights enforcement policies, are being tracked by advocacy groups and watchdogs.
However, the full implementation of priorities for 2025/2026 is expected to depend on congressional cooperation and potential legal challenges.
"The Chinese Communist Party is the most persistent and consequential foreign threat facing the American people today. It is building a military to challenge the U.S., stealing American technology at an unprecedented rate, and hurting U.S. workers with predatory economic policies. America is falling behind in this New Cold War.
"Heritage will push for decoupling key supply chains and key sectors of the U.S. economy from Chinese influence. Heritage will also expose U.S. firms lobbying for Chinese clients and work to end the flow of fentanyl from China that is killing 100,000 Americans per year. We will advance policies to confront China’s attack on American interests and values, develop a China-resistant economy, and ensure our military is prepared to meet any threats China may pose in the future."
"Government spending, regulations, and inflation are a tax on all Americans, especially working families who struggle to make ends meet. Prudent fiscal decisions can enable American families to flourish without politicians and bureaucrats controlling our lives. Reforming environmental and energy regulations will result in more affordable electricity and transportation and attract manufacturing back to America.
"Upholding the rule of law is paramount for a sovereign nation and orderly society. The American people deserve to live peacefully without incursions of illegal aliens across our borders. A strong immigration system enforces U.S. law, secures our borders, and preserves our national integrity. Yet, the Biden Administration intentionally and 'fundamentally transformed' America’s border and immigration system with lawlessness and incalculable and unsustainable costs, both human and fiscal.
"The Heritage enterprise will take steps at the federal and state level to restore the rule of law and provide messaging to encourage enforcement of the law."
"The right to vote in free and fair elections is the most basic civil right. Americans can have confidence in election outcomes only if they trust that the process is fair.
"Heritage will restore faith in elections and protect the right to vote for all Americans by advocating for commonsense reforms at the state and federal level like requiring proof of U.S. citizenship to vote and ending the practice of ranked-choice voting. We will always oppose a federal takeover of our elections by Washington elites."
"The education system is failing our children—from the scourge of woke ideas like critical race theory and radical gender ideology to the lack of academic excellence and transparency. Parents, not bureaucrats, should be making decisions about their children’s education, aligned with their values. Heritage is focused on loosening Washington’s grip on K-12 and higher education. Heritage will work to safeguard parental authority over their children’s education, expand universal education choice, protect American taxpayers from bailouts of an academia wholly adrift, and reclaim institutions of higher education from the radical Left.
"Heritage will spearhead reforms at the state level to expand education choice while working to limit federal intervention in education, with the goal of ultimately eliminating the U.S. Department of Education."
"American tech companies have been a source of amazing achievements for America, but in recent years, Big Tech has too often behaved as an enemy of the people, actively undermining American greatness. Big Tech has interfered with our elections, sought to groom and brainwash our children with perverted left-wing ideologies, engaged in anti-competitive behavior that stifles innovation, colluded with authoritarian governments, and sided with woke elites to suppress free speech and disproportionately silence conservatives.
"The Heritage enterprise will put power back in the hands of the American people by holding Big Tech accountable by enforcing antitrust and other laws and proposing new measures to end its abuses. We will advance laws and regulations that foster competition and cutting-edge innovation, making America’s tech sector the world’s strongest, most competitive, and most attractive for investment, while making it clear that the era of Big Tech impunity is over."
"Every child conceived deserves to be born to a married mother and father who will love, guide, and protect them throughout their lives. But family breakdown and rampant abortion tears at the soul of our country and saps it of strength and moral authority. Radical ideologies that deny social and biological truths about sexual embodiment, marriage, and unborn life poison our courts, our culture, and our laws.
"The Heritage enterprise will advance policies at the state and federal level to restore the nuclear family to the center of American life and to reduce both the demand and supply for abortion at all stages of human development with courage and compassion."
"A 'deep state' of unelected bureaucrats and special interest groups regularly bypasses the will of the people by undermining or overriding decisions made by elected officials. This erodes the American public’s trust in their own government. Heritage will support the new administration in its effort to assert effective control over the federal bureaucracy, rein in and reform the administrative state, end the abuses to Americans’ civil liberties, and restore excellence and efficiency in government operations.
"Heritage will work to reinvigorate the Constitution’s separation of powers and insist on democratic control and accountability in the federal government, for example by opposing any expansion of authority for independent agencies. Heritage will also fight the power of special interest groups that seek protection from competition in the form of government-created cartels and monopolies."
"Energy fuels human flourishing and is the foundation of the modern economy. Policies that limit oil and natural gas production weaken America’s domestic energy production and make it more costly, while requiring the use of solar and wind energy makes America dependent on China, raises electricity costs, disproportionately harms poor people and farmers, and undermines America’s economic growth.
"A historic electricity scarcity crisis is looming on the horizon, threatening reliable and affordable electricity. To avert the crisis, Heritage will advance policies that streamline infrastructure permitting, repeal the toxic renewables subsidies of the Inflation Reduction Act, and reform electricity market regulations."
Correction 12/9/25, 6:15 p.m. ET: This article was corrected to clarify language used by The Heritage Foundation to describe its 2026 priorities.

title: New Yorker Films Joe Jonas Struggling to Parallel-Park—He Claps Back Online
description: A viral TikTok video has sparked a wave of jokes after a New York City resident captured the moment she watched singer Joe Jonas try to parallel-park for seven minutes.
content:
A viral TikTok video has sparked a wave of jokes after a New York City resident captured the moment she watched singer Joe Jonas try to parallel-park for seven minutes.
The New York because I’ve been watching Joe Jonas parallel park for the last 7 minutes." It played into a familiar city scenario where a driver faces a tight curbside spot and an audience forms.
Celebrity "caught in the wild" moments often gain fast attention online, especially when they revolve around everyday frustrations.
The Zebra insurance company found that about 49 percent of Americans experience what researchers dub "parallelophobia," the fear of parallel parking.
The anxiety is understandable. The maneuver demands slow-speed precision, spatial awareness and the confidence to perform under the gaze of impatient traffic.
That pressure can lead to rushed attempts and misjudged angles, increasing the likelihood of scrapes, curb strikes and other vehicle damage.
Not everyone struggles with parallel parking, though. In January 2015, the current Guinness World Record for the tightest parallel park was set. Alastair Moffatt, from the U.K., was driving a Fiat 500C when he performed the "tightest" parallel park on record at 2.95 inches.
Joe Jonas is not the first person to go viral for a stressful parking attempt; in 2021, a woman went viral after spending minutes battling with a parking space—and a member of the public even offered to stand in and help.
"He’s a real one for not giving up, most of us would’ve just left," one commenter wrote, while another added, "the worst part of parallel parking is always the witnesses."
"Ngl [I'm not going to lie] I would cry if someone filmed me struggling to parallel park," said a third TikTok user in the comments. One viewer wrote: "You missed the perfect opportunity to parallel park Joe Jonas' car for him."

title: Twins Arrested After ‘Terroristic Threats’ to Kill Trump Admin Official
description: Two New Jersey men have been arrested after making terroristic threats to Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Assistant Secretary Tricia McLaughlin.
content:
Two New Jersey men have been arrested after making terroristic threats to Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Assistant Secretary Tricia McLaughlin.
Emilio Roman-Flores and Ricardo Antonio Roman-Flores, who are twins from Abescon, are accused of threatening to shoot ICE officers "on sight" in addition to threatening to hang McLaughlin, according to a press release emailed to Newsweek DHS on Tuesday.
McLaughlin has become one of the key faces of the Department of Homeland Security under President Donald Trump, alongside Secretary Kristi Noem. As a leading spokesperson, she has repeatedly raised concerns about a rise in attacks on and threats towards federal agents, while also putting her name alongside statements targeting alleged illegal immigrants and policies of the previous administration, as well as current Democratic Party leaders in sanctuary cities and states.
DHS Assistant Secretary for Public Affairs Tricia McLaughlin speaks during a news conference at ICE Headquarters in Washington on May 21, 2025. | AP Photo/Jose Luis Magana, File
Emilio Roman-Flores is being charged with unlawful possession of an assault weapon, possession of prohibited weapons, conspiracy, terroristic threats, criminal coercion, threats, and cyber harassment, while his twin, Ricardo Antonio Roman-Flores, is charged with conspiracy, terroristic threats, the release said.
The pair is being held in a New Jersey jail and faces federal charges, according to the release.
"We Americans should find you, tar you, feather you, and hang you as we did to anyone serving tyrants before the Revolutionary War," one post allegedly said, per Fox News.
Federal agents have seen an uptick in attacks in recent months, DHS has said, as they increased immigration enforcement across the country at the direction of President Donald Trump. Protests have broken out in several communities, with confrontations between residents and agents frequently documented.
ICE agents have also been accused of using aggressive tactics against citizens and alleged illegal immigrants, leaving some with serious injuries.
"The extreme rhetoric of the news media, sanctuary politicians, and activists is leading directly to our law enforcement officers facing an 8,000% increase in death threats against them. If you threaten our law enforcement or DHS officials, we will hunt you down and you will be prosecuted to the fullest extent of the law.”
Further details on the case are pending, while DHS has made it clear it will seek to prosecute all of those who threaten agents and staff.
Update 12/9/25, 1:31 p.m. ET: This article was updated with a response from the Department of Homeland Security.

title: Putin Ally Issues Update on Deploying Nuclear Weapons in NATO Warning
description: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko shared an update on Tuesday about his plans to deploy nuclear weapons in coordination with Russia throughout his country.
content:
The close ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin said that "the question of returning nuclear weapons to us was raised."
"We also discussed putting on alert Russia’s state-of-the-art systems. We have full mutual understanding and support from Russia. Therefore, we will work together to defend our country," Lukashenko said, according to Belarus' BelTA news agency.
"We cannot ignore this very mobilization and militarization [by NATO nations]," Lukashenko said. "These countries are not spending vast amounts of money on, as they say, countering Russia and Belarus for no reason. We do not intend to attack anyone. We have enough of everything."
"We need to update our defense measures so that everyone sees we are ready to defend every patch of our land," he warned.
Russia's President Vladimir Putin (R) greets Belarus' President Alexander Lukashenko at the Kremlin in Moscow on September 26, 2025. | RAMIL SITDIKOV/POOL/AFP via Getty Images
Russia originally announced plans to place tactical nuclear weapons in Belarus in March 2023, at Minsk’s request. By April 2024, Lukashenko confirmed the delivery of several dozen warheads. The weapons fall under the framework of the Union State of Russia and Belarus, which came into effect in March 2025 and includes a provision that such arms can be used only as a measure of last resort.
Lukashenko also confirmed that Russia’s Oreshnik intermediate-range ballistic missile system will enter combat duty in Belarus in December and will be deployed across the country. The Oreshnik system, which was first publicly revealed during a 2024 strike on the Ukrainian city of Dnipro, is capable of delivering both conventional and nuclear payloads.
“Just so you know, we have once again exchanged our nuclear weapons,” Lukashenko said, according to BelTA. “We sent them to Russia and brought back the most advanced ones. They were put in order. Maintaining these weapons is expensive, and the Russians help us with that. So we returned these weapons. We train using them, both from aircraft and missile weapons. And we make no secret of it.”
In September, Russian and Belarusian forces conducted joint exercises under “Zapad 2025,” a recurring military drill series that included simulations of nonstrategic nuclear operations. The exercises involved roughly 30,000 troops—far fewer than the 100,000 claimed by Moscow—according to Lithuanian intelligence cited by the ELTA news agency. Only 2,000 of those troops were Russian; the rest were from Belarus and other participating nations.
Despite the smaller scale, the drills were described by Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk as “very aggressive.” In response, Poland conducted its own war games, code-named Iron Defender, which included 30,000 troops and forces from multiple NATO member states.
With the Oreshnik missile system set to enter combat duty in December and tactical warheads already deployed, Western officials could press for new security measures, increased surveillance, or even consultations under Article 4 of the NATO treaty.
Update 12/9/25, 12:21 p.m. ET: This article was updated with additional information.

title: Pioneering Precision Surgery: The Da Vinci Robotic Surgery Program at KMUH
description: In the era of constant innovation in surgical care, robotic-assisted surgery has become a defining hallmark of modern surgical technology. Kaohsiung Medical University Chung-Ho Memorial Hospital (KMUH) stands at the forefront of this transf
content:
In the era of constant innovation in surgical care, robotic-assisted surgery has become a defining hallmark of modern surgical technology. Kaohsiung Medical University Chung-Ho Memorial Hospital (KMUH) stands at the forefront of this transformation in Taiwan. As a leading authority in colorectal surgery and cancer molecular medicine, I've also been recognized as a key opinion leader in minimally invasive robotic colorectal surgery in Taiwan.
Since 2013, I have performed over 800 Da Vinci robotic surgeries, becoming the first to achieve this milestone and maintaining my leadership to date in Taiwan. These efforts have turned KMUH into a major teaching and training hub, drawing both domestic and international physicians and significantly raising the hospital’s global visibility.
KMUH was one of the earliest institutions in Taiwan to introduce the fourth-generation Da Vinci Xi system, which significantly enhances the precision, flexibility and safety of surgical procedures. With smaller incisions, less pain and faster recovery, patients experience significantly improved postoperative outcomes and quality of life. Unique high dissection and selective ligation technique of colorectal cancer were first proposed by Prof. Wang’s team. With our outstanding achievement, KMUH was selected as the first observation center for robotic colorectal surgery in Taiwan.
To further enhance recovery and patient prognosis, we have also integrated the principles of ERAS (Enhanced Recovery After Surgery) into our robotic surgery protocols since 2013. This multidisciplinary approach emphasizes early mobilization, reduced invasive interventions and streamlined care pathways, resulting in shorter hospital stays and reduced complications.
Robotic arms are not about showcasing technology—they are about transforming patient outcomes. At KMUH, surgical excellence goes hand in hand with cancer genomic testing and personalized treatment, fulfilling the hospital’s commitment to patient-centered precision medicine.
KMUH is the only facility in Southern Taiwan equipped with a dual-surgeon Da Vinci console and an integrated surgical table system.
The reach of KMUH’s robotic surgery program extends beyond Taiwan. We have become a learning destination for physicians across Asia. Advancing studies among the international academic community is also important, so I have published over 420 peer-reviewed papers, obtained 12 patents and led multiple clinical innovation trials.
I also believe strongly in interdisciplinary collaboration, launching cutting-edge platforms such as “smart operating rooms” and intraoperative real-time imaging analytics. These innovations have not only appeared in prestigious international journals but have also contributed to updated global clinical guidelines in colorectal surgery.
KMUH has consistently refined its robotic surgery outcomes and comprehensive care models. As a result, KMUH was ranked eighth in Taiwan in the 2025 Newsweek
The hospital also boasts a strong record of international accreditation. We are the only medical center in Taiwan to have passed the JCI (Joint Commission International) accreditation three times. In both 2021 and 2024, we received the Center of Excellence in Robotic Surgery (COERS) certification from the U.S.-based Surgical Review Corporation (SRC). In addition, we are officially designated as an international proctoring and observation center for colorectal robotic surgery—making us a critical hub for surgical education and skill transfer in the Asia-Pacific region.
These domestic and global recognitions not only affirm my leadership in precision surgery but also underscore KMUH’s role as a beacon of medical innovation in southern Taiwan.
In December 2023, the Da Vinci Si surgical system, in service for over 10 years, was decommissioned. | Courtesy Kaohsiung Medical University Chung-Ho Memorial Hospital
Looking ahead, I am committed to integrating AI and genomic analysis into clinical decision-making. We aim to provide a novel, highly personalized surgical planning tailored to each patient’s unique profile. Expansion into hepatobiliary, urology and gynecology surgeries is also underway, alongside the establishment of an AI Surgical Analytics Center, which will use data platforms to predict risk and optimize surgical workflows.
The Da Vinci robotic system is just the beginning. True innovation lies not only in the tools themselves, but also in the meaningful changes they bring to patients’ lives. At KMUH, each robotic arm is more than a piece of advanced equipment—it is an “extended hand” offering hope and transforming the future of care.

title: Religion’s Long Decline in America May Be Plateauing
description: After decades of steady decline, new research indicates that the number of Americans identifying as religious may have stabilized, signaling a possible plateau in the nation’s ongoing religious transformation.
content:
After decades of steady decline, new research indicates that the number of Americans identifying as religious may have stabilized, signaling a possible plateau in the nation’s ongoing religious transformation.
This is a "striking" change from the past few decades, which have seen "measures of religious belonging, behaving and believing" dropping across the country, Pew’s Senior Associate Director of Research Gregory A. Smith said.
The stabilization of religious identification in the United States has broad implications for social, cultural and political life, especially for Christianity, with which 62 percent of Americans identify, according to Pew's 2023-2024 Religious Landscape Study.
For generations, Christianity has played a central role in American society, shaping everything from voting behavior to community organizations.
A plateau in religious decline could mean that the anticipated transformation of American religious and civic life may slow, as the pace of generational change levels off.
A woman holds a U.S, flag as she waits for the start of the Angelus noon prayer led by Pope Francis in St. Peter's Square in the Vatican on September ...Read More | AP Photo/Gregorio Borgia
Key measures of religiousness have remained remarkably stable since 2020 with around 70 percent of U.S. adults identifying with a religion. Specific indicators—such as rates of daily prayer, perceived importance of religion and regular service attendance—have shown little change year-over-year.
These found that there has been a 17-percent drop in the number of U.S. adults who say religion is an important part of their daily life between 2015, when it was 66 percent, and 2025, when it is 49 percent.
In September, a study concluded that the U.S. is in the "second stage" of a transition from being religious to secular—when "the importance of religion declines in people's personal lives," according to the paper "The Three Stages of Religious Decline Around the World."
The new Pew polls show that young adults continue to be less religious, with 55 percent of those between the ages of 23 and 30 identifying with a religion—similar to 57 percent in 2020.
Comparatively, 83 percent of the oldest adults identify with a religion, according to Pew, a figure that is also a similar to what it was in 2020—84 percent.
This divide is especially clear among religious practices—59 percent of the oldest Americans say they pray every day while 30 percent of the youngest adults say the same.
Some 43 percent of the oldest Americans say they go to religious services at least once a month while 26 percent of the youngest Americans say they attend services at least monthly.
"These patterns may reflect the fact that many of the youngest adults still live in their childhood homes and follow the religious customs of their families," Pew author Smith said. "As they get older and more of them leave home, their religious habits may change."
"Thus, we shouldn’t assume that the religiousness of today’s youngest adults is a sign of a major shift in American religion," he continued. "Perhaps in the future we’ll look back and see that we were at a pivotal moment in 2025," he said. "But historical data suggests the patterns we see today are a normal result of the youngest adults possibly following the religiousness of their parents for a few years past the age of 18, after which their religiousness begins to drop."
He added: "This secular transition isn't completely uniform, and it may not be inevitable everywhere."
Scholars caution that the United States may remain near this new level of religious affiliation for years, but the long-term outcome is still uncertain.
Researchers and faith leaders will continue to monitor whether the current stability endures or gives way to renewed shifts in American religious affiliation, practice, and political engagement.

title: Soup Recall Map Shows New Warning in 14 States
description: Lil’ Turtles is recalling Grandma Belle’s Tomato Basil Soup due to a milk allergen not being listed on the label.
content:
Venezuela Government Orders Capture of People Who Supported U.S. Operations
Lil’ Turtles is recalling Grandma Belle’s Tomato Basil Soup due to a milk allergen not being listed on the label.
According to the FDA alert, all lot numbers of the soup product are impacted in the recall. The product was packaged in 17-ounce glass jars and was available in retail stores from September 23, 2022, to December 3, 2025.
The jars were sold in certain retail stores in Ohio, Pennsylvania, New York, Kentucky, Minnesota, Utah, Oklahoma, Kansas, Mississippi, Illinois, Indiana, Missouri, Wisconsin, and Iowa.
Customers who have purchased the recalled soup and have a sensitivity or allergy to milk are urged to contact Lil’ Turtles for a new pack, the alert says. People with additional questions about the recall may contact the company at 330-897-6400.
Who Is William DeFoor? 26-Year-Old Accused of Damaging JD Vance’s Home
Numerous public health alerts and recalls have been issued this year due to potential damage, foodborne illness, contamination and undeclared food allergens.
Millions of Americans experience food sensitivities or allergies every year. According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the nine "major" food allergens in the United States are eggs, milk, fish, wheat, soybeans, crustacean shellfish, sesame, tree nuts and peanuts.
Grandma Belle’s Tomato Basil Soup, which was recalled in December 2025. | U.S. Food and Drug Administration
"The FDA's role during a voluntary, firm-initiated, recall is to review the recall strategy, evaluate the health hazard presented by the product, monitor the recall, and as appropriate alert the public and other companies in the supply chain about the recall."
